---
title: "var_power"
generated: true
description: "Power-of-covariate residual variance function (`nlme` `varPower`)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.stats.lme`](../nitrix-stats-lme.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats.lme._varfunc`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:var_power -->
```python
var_power(v: Float[Array, 'N']) -> VarFunc
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:var_power -->
Power-of-covariate residual variance function (`nlme` `varPower`).

Models the residual scale as a power of an external covariate,
$\operatorname{Var}(\varepsilon_i) = \sigma_e^2 |v_i|^{2 \delta}$.
`v` is passed explicitly, so the fit stays non-circular -- it is *not* the
fitted value.  There is one unconstrained parameter $\delta$ (any real;
$\delta = 0$ recovers homoscedasticity), with $\log g_i = \delta
\log|v_i|$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:var_power -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `v` | `Float` | `N` | — | The `(N,)` variance covariate, one value per observation. An external (non-circular) covariate such as a baseline level; its magnitude $\|v_i\|$ drives the residual scale. |

**Returns** `VarFunc`. A variance-function record named `'varPower'` with a single parameter, carrying `v` as its covariate and the $\log g_i = \delta \log|v_i|$ log-scale map.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:var_power -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
